promotionRule.secondRepeat: Add a promise candidate or explain why it cannot be deterministic yet.
promotionRule.thirdRepeat: Promote the promise or record the missing primitive, access, or observability that blocks promotion.

promises.claim: The self-improving harness promise registry is structurally valid.
promises.whenToUse: After editing docs/context/HARNESS_PROMISES.json or the harness contract.
passRequires: registry JSON parses
promise ids are unique and stable
required fields are present
evidence classes and cost values are canonical
report JSON has ok true
nextSuggestedSteps: Fix the first reported registry error and rerun the validator.

promises.claim: The self-improving harness declares five bounded loops with independent candidate adjudication, nine-harness same-model benchmarking, mandatory regression protection, and gated improvement.
promises.whenToUse: After changing the five-loop contract, fixture-bank lifecycle, benchmark matrix, regression policy, or improvement termination rules.
passRequires: the contract declares exactly five ordered loops
candidate, classifier-dispute, golden, regression, and insight banks are distinct
redaction, bounded trajectory windows, immutable replay, and provenance are mandatory
a cycle repeats only for new evidence, a materially different hypothesis, or measurable improvement
loop three requires nine comparable parallel harness slots on frank/GLM-5.2
loop four permits zero safety or grounded-completion regressions
loop five generates nine isolated parallel GLM-5.2 V5 variants
every loop-five variant requires its own digest-bound loop-four proof before ranking
loop five requires explicit resource bounds, no-progress patience, and typed terminal outcomes
nextSuggestedSteps: Fix the first structural error without weakening a loop bound or independent adjudication rule.
If the contract passes, implement candidate capture and replay as separate owned modules.

promises.claim: The safe lab launches nine isolated sibling containers for Loop 3 and nine for Loop 5, proves concurrent overlap and immutable input equality, and forbids ranking simulated adapters.
promises.whenToUse: After changing safe-lab lane isolation, adapter registry, Loop 3/5 orchestration, result collection, concurrency proof, or ranking gates.
passRequires: benchmark and improve modes each launch exactly nine unique sibling containers
every lane has a unique writable workspace and result volume
all lanes observe the same source digest and 400-fixture bank
at least two lanes overlap on the bounded proof host
Loop 5 uses nine distinct predeclared strategy hypotheses
simulated topology lanes remain non-comparable and ranking is forbidden
proof-owned volumes are removed after result validation
nextSuggestedSteps: Fix the first isolation, concurrency, fixture equality, or ranking-gate failure and rerun the promise.
Do not treat topology proof as a live agent benchmark; live mode requires nine verified adapters and brokered GLM-5.2 receipts.

promises.claim: Packaged Hermes reaches exact frank/GLM-5.2 only through the private broker and can use canonical terminal authority to mutate only its private workspace.
promises.whenToUse: After changing Hermes packaging, the model gateway, duplicate policy, live adapter configuration, tool authority, or lane containment.
passRequires: the lane reaches the broker but cannot reach the upstream provider directly
every upstream receipt reports exact frank/GLM-5.2 over SSE
the provider credential is absent from the lane
source and fixture mounts are read-only while the private workspace is writable
runtime sockets and host block devices are absent
Hermes emits a terminal tool call and creates the exact nonce-bound workspace artifact
no identical request reaches upstream more than twice and a third is blocked outside explicit benchmark scenarios
all proof-owned containers, networks, volumes, and temporary credential files are removed
nextSuggestedSteps: Adjudicate a duplicate warning against gateway transport evidence before blaming the adapter.
Keep Hermes non-rankable until the generic nine-lane fixture task and broker wiring are complete.

promises.claim: A redacted SQL fixture is materialized into a digest-bound task and executed through a generic brokered adapter lane with hard model, authority, call, duplicate, time, output, cleanup, and ranking gates.
promises.whenToUse: After changing fixture materialization, live adapter commands, lane execution, model-gateway budgets, source-volume routing, result validation, or readiness promotion fields.
passRequires: the selected fixture comes from the immutable 400-candidate SQL bank and has a stable task digest
missing required route surfaces block before any provider call
the lane receives only a run-scoped broker token and cannot reach the upstream provider directly
every upstream call reports exact frank/GLM-5.2
the gateway blocks a third identical request and any provider call above the task total-call ceiling
adapter turns, wall clock, answer bytes, and provider calls remain within the task budgets
the lane writes a bounded result even on timeout
semantic correctness marked unresolved keeps ranking disabled
all proof-owned containers, networks, volumes, and temporary credential files are removed
nextSuggestedSteps: Reuse the independently adjudicated semantic slice for every adapter readiness proof.
Implement the next adapter against the same task, authority, broker, and result contract.

promises.claim: A private immutable overlay admits a deduplicated golden/holdout slice only after two independent checks agree, exposes only contract digests to lanes, and gates ranking on trusted post-run semantic scoring.
promises.whenToUse: After changing fixture selection, semantic properties, holdout policy, materialization, live scoring, safe-lab seed exclusions, or adapter benchmark readiness.
passRequires: the overlay digest matches the registered semantic fixture contract and immutable SQL bank
all selected objectives are deduplicated and retain pending status in the 400-candidate source bank
each admitted fixture has agreeing eligibility and task-stability judgments independent of the preliminary classifier
the slice contains seven golden and two holdout fixtures, including grounded source and runtime fixtures with balanced tool and route evidence
raw runtime identifiers, secrets, historical reply bodies, expected properties, and holdout criteria do not enter lane tasks
trusted post-run scoring matches the digest projected into the task
benchmark readiness requires both technical and semantic proof
nextSuggestedSteps: Prove the next adapter against the identical admitted task and semantic scorer.
Do not launch a nine-way ranking run until every adapter has matching benchmark readiness proof.

promises.claim: Master:frontier V5 is a minimal immutable adapter artifact that preserves owned source layout, passes a no-network import preflight before provider work, and earns benchmark readiness only through the common live semantic gate.
promises.whenToUse: After changing V5 owned modules, its safe-lab runner, adapter packaging, registry metadata, or common live fixture execution.
passRequires: the package contains only the runner and V5 dependency closure
the package receipt binds every included file and contains no credentials or runtime state
the source-relative plugins/wasm-agent/server depth is preserved
a no-network unprivileged cache-hit preflight imports and executes master_frontier.v5.loop
the live lane receives the same task, model, tool authority, budgets, and private semantic scorer used by other adapters
exact frank/GLM-5.2, broker-only access, direct-egress denial, bounded output, semantic success, and cleanup are proven before benchmark promotion
nextSuggestedSteps: Package the next harness behind the identical task, broker, authority, result, and semantic contracts.
Keep the nine-way benchmark blocked until every registry slot has matching proof.

promises.claim: The real Codex CLI runs from an immutable credential-free package with ephemeral state and reaches the common GLM-5.2 broker through a bounded Responses compatibility bridge before earning semantic benchmark readiness.
promises.whenToUse: After changing the installed Codex artifact, Codex runner/config, Responses bridge, model gateway, package receipt, or Codex registry readiness.
passRequires: the package contains the installed Codex distribution and runner but no Codex home, auth, config, sessions, memories, plugins, or MCP credentials
a no-network unprivileged package preflight reports codex-cli 0.144.0-alpha.4
the runner uses an ephemeral isolated Codex home and the externally-sandboxed automation mode inside the outer safe-lab boundary
the custom provider uses the supported Responses wire API and only a run-scoped broker token
the broker deterministically translates Responses text, function calls, prior tool outputs, and exact usage while retaining the common duplicate and total-call admission gate
the live lane proves exact frank/GLM-5.2, direct-egress denial, bounded output, semantic success, and cleanup before benchmark promotion
nextSuggestedSteps: Reuse the common broker admission and semantic gate for the next harness protocol.
Keep readiness observations separate from Loop 3 multi-fixture ranking.

promises.claim: The real Claude Code CLI runs from an immutable credential-free package with ephemeral state and reaches the common GLM-5.2 broker through bounded Anthropic Messages compatibility before earning semantic benchmark readiness.
promises.whenToUse: After changing the installed Claude Code artifact, runner environment, Anthropic bridge, model gateway, package receipt, efficiency warnings, or Claude registry readiness.
passRequires: the package contains the installed Claude Code binary, runner, and version receipt but no host auth, config, sessions, memories, or runtime state
a no-network unprivileged package preflight reports Claude Code 2.1.207
the runner uses ephemeral isolated home and config directories with bare, safe, non-persistent execution inside the outer safe-lab boundary
the Anthropic Messages and local count_tokens surfaces receive only a run-scoped broker credential
the broker translates bounded text, tools, tool results, streaming events, and exact usage while retaining the common duplicate and total-call admission gate
the live lane proves exact frank/GLM-5.2, direct-egress denial, bounded output, semantic success, and cleanup before benchmark promotion
unnecessary tools on a self-contained conversation preserve success but emit a machine-readable efficiency warning with bounded tool names
nextSuggestedSteps: Benchmark a generic task-level tool-necessity contract against both self-contained and tool-required fixtures.
Keep the warning non-fatal until Loop 3 evidence proves a safe cost improvement.

promises.claim: The official Gemini CLI runs from an immutable credential-free package with ephemeral state and reaches the common GLM-5.2 broker through bounded Google GenerateContent compatibility before earning semantic benchmark readiness.
promises.whenToUse: After changing the Gemini CLI version, pinned Node runtime, runner auth/config, GenerateContent bridge, model gateway, package receipt, efficiency warnings, or Gemini registry readiness.
passRequires: the package contains integrity-pinned @google/gemini-cli 0.50.0, a pinned Node 22 runtime, and the runner but no host Gemini auth, config, conversations, memory, installation id, or runtime state
a no-network unprivileged package preflight reports Gemini CLI 0.50.0 and the complete package tree matches its immutable digest
the runner uses private ephemeral home/config paths and selects Gemini API-key auth only inside that home to avoid the 0.50.0 headless gateway-validator defect
GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL remains the private broker and GEMINI_API_KEY contains only the run-scoped broker token
GenerateContent text, tools, function calls, function results, streaming, exact usage, and local countTokens retain common duplicate and total-call admission
the live lane proves exact frank/GLM-5.2, direct-egress denial, bounded output, semantic success, and cleanup before benchmark promotion
disproportionate prompt context on a self-contained conversation preserves success but emits a machine-readable efficiency warning
nextSuggestedSteps: Benchmark context-loading reductions against both self-contained and repository-grounded fixtures before altering Gemini defaults.
Keep the context-cost warning non-fatal until Loop 3 evidence proves a safe improvement.

promises.claim: Official Aider runs from an immutable credential-free Python package, reaches exact GLM-5.2 through the common Chat broker, and emits only its final assistant message before earning semantic benchmark readiness.
promises.whenToUse: After changing the Aider version, package builder, runner flags, OpenAI-compatible endpoint, history answer shaping, registry metadata, or Aider readiness proof.
passRequires: the package contains official aider-chat 0.86.2 and its dependency-locked isolated venv plus the runner but no host config, history, secrets, or runtime state
the complete package tree and dependency freeze match immutable digests and an offline unprivileged preflight reports Aider 0.86.2
only package bootstrap writes a new root-owned volume as root while preflight and live execution remain UID 10000
the runner uses one-shot openai/glm-5.2 through the private OPENAI_API_BASE with only the run-scoped broker token
git, commits, analytics, updates, browser tooling, streaming, host history, and interactive confirmations are disabled
only the final assistant role parsed from private bounded chat history becomes the benchmark answer
the live lane proves exact frank/GLM-5.2, direct-egress denial, bounded output, semantic success, and cleanup before benchmark promotion
nextSuggestedSteps: Reuse the existing Chat broker and answer-shaping gate for the next compatible harness.
Keep CLI diagnostics as evidence artifacts, never as semantic answer content.

promises.claim: Official OpenCode runs from an immutable architecture-specific native package, reaches exact GLM-5.2 through the common Chat broker, and emits only completed structured text events before earning semantic benchmark readiness.
promises.whenToUse: After changing the OpenCode version, platform package, preflight state, runner config, structured event parsing, registry metadata, or OpenCode readiness proof.
passRequires: the package contains integrity-pinned opencode-linux-arm64 1.17.18 and the runner but no host auth, config, sessions, plugins, secrets, or runtime state
npm lifecycle scripts remain disabled and the architecture-specific native binary passes an offline unprivileged version preflight
the preflight root and adapter remain read-only while config, data, and cache use only a bounded disposable tmpfs home
pure one-shot lab/glm-5.2 uses @ai-sdk/openai-compatible and only the run-scoped broker token
remote model fetches, plugins, Claude state import, LSP downloads, updates, sharing, and internet tools are disabled
only completed structured text events become the benchmark answer
the live lane proves exact frank/GLM-5.2, direct-egress denial, bounded output, semantic success, and cleanup before benchmark promotion
multiple distinct calls without a tool loop on a self-contained conversation preserve success but emit the shared auxiliary-call warning
nextSuggestedSteps: Benchmark deterministic session metadata as a cost hypothesis without changing the raw readiness evidence.
Reuse direct platform packaging when a cross-platform wrapper requires lifecycle scripts.

promises.claim: Successful self-contained conversation runs retain semantic success while shared deterministic policy emits bounded warnings for unnecessary tool use and disproportionate prompt context.
promises.whenToUse: After changing live receipt fields, conversation request classes, efficiency thresholds, warning fixtures, adapter warning metadata, or Loop 2/3 cost adjudication.
passRequires: a self-contained conversation with tool calls emits unnecessary_tool_use_for_self_contained_conversation with bounded unique tool names
a self-contained conversation above eight times its output allowance emits excessive_prompt_context_for_self_contained_conversation
multiple distinct provider calls without a tool loop on a self-contained conversation emit unnecessary_auxiliary_provider_call_for_self_contained_conversation
both warning classes preserve semantic success and ranking eligibility
repository-grounded and other non-self-contained request classes are excluded from these heuristics
the checker report has ok true
nextSuggestedSteps: Adjudicate warning frequency and cost deltas across the full Loop 3 fixture matrix before changing a harness default.
Promote a warning to an error only when a declared hard budget or safety contract is violated.

promises.claim: Durable context docs agree with docs/context/ACTIVE_STATE.json and current proof-order rules.
promises.whenToUse: After context edits or before trusting durable next actions from docs.
passRequires: context sync command exits 0
reports/context/latest/context-sync-result.json has ok true
no stale current next actions are reported
nextSuggestedSteps: Run with --fix only for generated ACTIVE_STATE drift.
Demote or rewrite stale claims instead of trusting narrative text.

promises.claim: The active agent loop has no cheap local loop-copilot blocker before slow runtime work, rebuild-heavy work, or final claims.
promises.whenToUse: Before meaningful native, bridge, wake-word, hot-op, runtime-control, release, rebuild-heavy work, or final claims.
passRequires: loop copilot command exits 0
reports/context/latest/loop-copilot-signals.json has ok true
no blocker signal is present
the worker treats any warning as checkpoint guidance, not runtime proof
nextSuggestedSteps: If a blocker is emitted, run the named proof or demote the claim before proceeding.
If only warnings are emitted, include the warning in Builder/Watcher/Gatekeeper review before final claims.
If runtime-control work is visible, prefer compact live introspection/control before rebuilding.

promises.claim: The Windows release feed matches the verified final NSIS installer metadata.
promises.whenToUse: Before asking Go Native or Check Update to see a Windows build.
passRequires: native/windows/release/VERIFY.json exists
feed buildId matches verified installer buildId
feed SHA-256 matches verified installer SHA-256
local published installer bytes match feed metadata
nextSuggestedSteps: If VERIFY.json is missing, rebuild and verify the final NSIS installer before claiming feed proof.
If the feed mismatches, regenerate the release feed from verified package metadata.

promises.claim: The installed Windows native-control bridge exposes the required hot-op shell capabilities.
promises.whenToUse: Before doctor, Android wake proof, or any ADB-backed Android work through the Windows bridge.
passRequires: installed Windows app is running
local bridge responds
shellProtocolVersion and hotOpsProtocolVersion match docs
required shell capabilities are present
canary hot operation returns success
nextSuggestedSteps: If bridge_update_required or hot_operation_missing appears, fix bridge/hot-op loading before blaming Android runtime.
If the installed app is absent, install the feed-published shell first.

promises.claim: The Android runtime behavior named by the simulator report passed.
promises.whenToUse: When a claim depends on Android runtime behavior rather than APK package metadata.
passRequires: simulator command exits 0
report names the exact behavior proven
report status is PASS
claim does not exceed the behavior named by the report
nextSuggestedSteps: If the simulator is unavailable, classify as invalid-environment instead of Android-disconnected.
Run a behavior-specific local report when reusing external device evidence.

promises.claim: Android Gradle rebuilds run the UX performance regression guard before build work proceeds.
promises.whenToUse: Before any Android rebuild, after changing Android startup/touch/shell code, or when checking that incremental native work cannot skip the UX regression guard.
passRequires: native/android/app/build.gradle declares verifyAndroidUxPerformanceRegression
preBuild depends on verifyAndroidUxPerformanceRegression
the guard command is plugins/wasm-agent/tests/android_lite_performance_budget.test.js
the guard includes Android shell v2 size, bridge, loadUrl, and forbidden-startup budgets
the guard requires NativeShellV2Activity to own the installed launcher/auth-return routes while legacy MainActivity is not the normal relaunch path
the guard requires Android input-budget proof to budget Activity launch timings
the guard requires the deterministic release loop to reuse the existing feed on --skip-build unless --publish-feed is explicit
the guard requires the deterministic release loop to accept install proof only when APK SHA/build diagnostics match the promoted release
the guard requires shell-v2 ADB relaunch proof to be explicit opt-in and launch-only without force-stop or synthetic swipe
the guard requires the UX release-loop install phase to use the Android UI input hot-op install action rather than the legacy voice-tuning reinstall command
Gradle :app:preBuild executes :app:verifyAndroidUxPerformanceRegression
the guard prints android lite performance budget ok
nextSuggestedSteps: If the promise fails, fix the guard or the Gradle preBuild hook before rebuilding.
If an Android feature needs to exceed a budget, split it into a later v2 layer with a new explicit gate instead of raising layer-0 budgets silently.
This promise is not runtime UX proof; after an installed APK change, still run android-native-input-budget or android-native-ux-release-loop.

promises.claim: Android native canvas/navigation input stays inside the configured frame/input budget after a real ADB swipe through the Windows bridge.
promises.whenToUse: When Android native feels laggy, after changing PWA/native interaction code, or before resuming wake-word tuning that depends on responsive UI.
passRequires: Windows native-control bridge is reachable
run_android_ui_input_proof hot operation is available
gfxinfo reset succeeds
Activity launch total/wait time is present and within the configured budget when launched through am start -W
real ADB swipe succeeds unless --launch-only is explicitly selected
post-swipe gfxinfo has high input latency, slow UI thread, missed frame deadline, slow draw-command, jank, and p95 values at or below configured budget unless --launch-only is explicitly selected
nextSuggestedSteps: Use --launch-component com.colmeio.wasmagent/.shell.NativeShellV2Activity only to isolate shell-v2 proof; normal app icon/relaunch proof must still resolve to shell v2.
If high input latency is nonzero, inspect event/listener/main-thread pressure before wake tuning.
If slow draw commands or missed deadlines dominate, simplify canvas paint/layout/scroll invalidation before adding more handlers.
If the bridge is unavailable, run windows-hot-shell-proof first instead of treating Android as disconnected.

promises.claim: The Android native UX release candidate was built or reused, published, installed through the Windows bridge, and proven against the real-device input budget as one deterministic loop.
promises.whenToUse: After Android native responsiveness source changes, before repeating any build/install/input-proof sequence, or whenever a source fix needs installed Android runtime proof.
passRequires: exactly one aggregate release-loop report is emitted under reports/android/responsiveness
local wasm-agent backend starts and /native/control/clients becomes reachable, or startup is explicitly skipped only when already reachable
the promoted APK/feed has a build id, SHA-256, and size
Windows hot-shell proof passes or is explicitly skipped only when already proven in the same active loop
--skip-build reuses the existing release feed unless --publish-feed is explicitly requested
shell-v2 proof blocks when the release feed still publishes an older Android UI input-proof hot-op without component launch support
shell-v2 mode does not perform a second ADB relaunch/input proof unless --run-shell-v2-adb-proof is explicit
the release-loop install phase uses run_android_ui_input_proof install_apk and does not perform a post-install monkey launch
explicit shell-v2 ADB proof is launch-only and avoids force-stop, swipe, and gfxinfo probes

the APK install through the Windows bridge passes, or the install phase/reused report shows matching APK SHA and Android build diagnostics for the promoted build, unless the run is explicitly --skip-install
real-device Android input-budget proof passes unless the run is explicitly --skip-input-budget
manual repeated rebuild/reinstall/proof commands are not used as the primary evidence for the same loop
nextSuggestedSteps: Use --skip-build only when the promoted APK/feed already contains the source change under test.
Do not refresh the feed during --skip-build unless --publish-feed is explicitly needed; feed refresh can invalidate reused install proof by changing APK metadata.
Use --reuse-install-report only when the named report proves the same promoted build id and APK SHA in Android diagnostics.
If this aggregate loop lacks a needed knob, add the knob to the script instead of hand-running the same sequence again.
If input budget fails after install, inspect the aggregate report metrics before rebuilding again.

promises.claim: The Android wake-word production loop runs on the installed shell-v2 launcher, starts wake through the app bridge, applies live policy, and routes a Windows synthesized wake phrase.
promises.whenToUse: When closing or tuning the Android wake-word production loop on shell v2, after shell-v2 APK/package proof exists, or before claiming wake-word shipped.
passRequires: release feed Android build id is android-universal-20260622T193436Z or newer
Windows hot-shell preflight passes through the installed Windows app
android-native-ux-release-loop runs with --skip-build --shell-v2 --run-shell-v2-adb-proof, or responsiveness is explicitly accepted with --accept-responsiveness-incomplete
shell-v2 control relaunch uses the local Windows bridge for ADB hot ops and a key-authorized Android command origin matching the WebView/control URL origin
shell-v2 control relaunch produces a fresh current-build Android WebView v2 native-control client on the Android command origin
installed Android wake state reports build id android-universal-20260622T193436Z or newer
wake is started through start_voice_wake / WasmAgentNative.enableVoiceWake rather than direct ADB service start
apply_wake_word_policy completes through the Android app bridge
refreshed wake state reports the foreground wake service active
Windows SpeechSynthesizer stimulus runs through play_wake_phrase_probe via tools/voice/run-wake-room-loop.py
room-loop timeline includes routed voice command evidence for the synthesized wake phrase
invalidatedBy: wake model or live policy change
nextSuggestedSteps: If preflight fails with handler_timeout, fully restart/reopen the installed Windows app and rerun.
If responsiveness proof fails, fix or explicitly accept that gate before wake tuning.
If wake state build id is stale, install android-universal-20260622T193436Z or newer through the Windows bridge.
If speech produces no timeline evidence, tune live wake policy/model without rebuilding unless a missing native primitive is proven.

promises.claim: wasm-agent route resolution and map/lookup tools are registry-backed, bounded by route contracts, and do not scan source during route.resolve.
promises.whenToUse: After changing wasm-agent route contracts, direct-head route resolution, route/map/lookup tools, or provider dispatch scoping.
passRequires: route.resolve returns a declared route contract for known wasm-agent surfaces
route.resolve missing-route behavior returns route_contract_missing without invoking source search
map.summary returns a tiny route summary
lookup.files returns bounded file receipts with path, bytes, and sha256 where available
lookup.symbol searches only the resolved route contract scope
explicit workspace_root does not bypass route contract resolution
nextSuggestedSteps: If this fails, fix the route registry or resolver before allowing provider/Hermes dispatch.
Do not add route ownership strings to static_server.py; add or repair a route contract.

promises.claim: static_server.py does not route wasm-agent product ownership through CSS selectors, DOM classes, product labels, or ad hoc route regex lists.
promises.whenToUse: After editing wasm-agent provider dispatch, route contracts, UI-symbol helpers, or static_server.py routing logic.
passRequires: static_server.py does not contain WASM_AGENT_UI_ROUTE_TERMS
static_server.py does not contain WASM_AGENT_UI_SELECTOR_RE
route ownership is represented by agent_route_contracts.json or context docs, not runtime selector branches
nextSuggestedSteps: If selector routing returns, remove it and add a route contract plus resolver test instead.

promises.claim: wasm-agent persists exact provider token usage by quest, turn, and provider call, with raw usage stored separately from normalized exact fields.
promises.whenToUse: After changing direct-head provider adapters, Hermes dispatch, agent run storage, token aggregation, or timeline token events.
passRequires: direct-head provider runs require a route contract before provider/Hermes dispatch
token ledger rows are persisted per provider call
quest/run total equals the sum of exact provider-call totals
raw provider usage remains available separately from normalized exact fields
cost.status exposes compact ledger totals
nextSuggestedSteps: If exact and estimated usage are mixed, split the fields before exposing totals.
If a provider omits usage, store estimated values only under estimated fields and mark exact false.

promises.claim: avatar-chat timeline and token-ledger UI remain width-contained with long route ids, token call ids, and event details.
promises.whenToUse: After changing avatar-chat timeline rendering, token ledger rendering, agent message CSS, or compact run proof UI.
passRequires: Chromium renders synthetic long timeline rows without document horizontal overflow
agent-timeline boxes stay inside the agent message bounds
agent-token-ledger boxes stay inside the agent message bounds
long provider call ids and route ids are clipped or wrapped inside bounded grid columns
nextSuggestedSteps: If this fails, fix the layout constraints before adding more timeline or ledger fields.
Do not hide overflow by removing route, token, or proof fields from the UI.

promises.claim: A simulator user can submit a two-turn avatar-chat UI quest and receive direct-head responses with route-before-provider proof, exact quest/turn/provider-call token ledger rows, no broad Hermes fallback, and contained run timeline UI.
promises.whenToUse: After changing avatar-chat submission, direct-head route preflight, provider dispatch, token ledger persistence, or compact run timeline UI.
passRequires: simulator admin user reaches the authenticated avatar-chat UI
the UI submits exactly two provider calls to the local Responses stub
route.resolved is recorded before head.started or provider dispatch evidence for every turn
route_contract_missing is returned for objective-only routing
the exact quest ledger input, output, and total token counts equal the sum of provider stub usage across turns
each turn ledger includes exact input, output, total, quest_id, turn_id, route_id, and provider_call_id
Hermes broad fallback is absent
timeline and token-ledger UI boxes are width-contained
nextSuggestedSteps: If route proof fails, fix the declarative route contract or preflight before provider dispatch.
If token totals fail, fix exact provider usage normalization and ledger persistence before exposing UI totals.
If containment fails, fix the timeline/token-ledger layout instead of hiding proof fields.
If the simulator cannot submit deterministically, stop with the missing UI primitive instead of dispatching Hermes broadly.

promises.claim: avatar-chat reflective/self-critique requests preserve the user's meta intent while keeping route proof intact, instead of collapsing into generic capability or status text.
promises.whenToUse: After changing direct-head envelope routing, intent classification, fallback response shaping, provider prompt projection, kernel proof projection, or avatar-chat submission for reflective/meta requests.
passRequires: a reflective request such as `critique your own envelope from within` resolves to `wasm-agent.avatar-chat.ui` before provider or fallback response composition
the task contract records a reflective/meta intent mode rather than only a UI route or runtime-inspection decision
the final response includes self-critique of the answering envelope, not just route/capability status
the final response names the generic failure class when route proof succeeds but user intent fidelity degrades
the final response gives a concrete improvement path such as a fixture, promise, or compact envelope field
the final response does not present route proof, source tests, or generic capability text as completion of the reflective request
nextSuggestedSteps: If route proof is missing, repair the declarative route contract or preflight before changing response wording.
If the answer becomes generic status text, add a reflective intent field to the compact task contract and prove response shaping from that field.
If the test only matches one prompt string, replace it with a small reflective-intent fixture class before expanding examples.

promises.claim: Master:frontier V3 exposes semantic operations to one model-led load-on-demand loop while keeping versioned C3 codes internal to receipts, with advisory token targets and explicit hard-budget admission.
promises.whenToUse: After changing the C3 registry, semantic-operation bootstrap, operation mapping, compact observations, provider continuation, budget admission, browser envelope, or V3 run finalization.
passRequires: the browser selects hermes.wasm_agent.master_frontier.v3 without objective-kind planning
the initial C3 bootstrap stays below the focused token ceiling and excludes verbose transport state
the bootstrap names C3 as the envelope, CSC/1 as continuity, and the registry digest as a digest without identity ambiguity
the model sees semantic operation names and arguments without the internal one-character tool dictionary
the head chooses one semantic operation at a time and the host maps it to a declared route-scoped canonical tool
cyphers remain present in persisted receipts and history while model continuation receives semantic operation and evidence projections
compact cypher JSON remains decode-only compatibility and unresolved operation-shaped output cannot leak as a final answer
bounded source observations preserve enough provider budget for a synthesis call
stateless provider continuation retains multiple prior evidence details inside one bounded context window
empty and unsupported tool results do not count as satisfying evidence
identical repeated tool calls stop before re-execution
default provider-token and API-call targets are advisory while explicitly hard budgets block before the next call
the output ceiling permits long requested answers without spending unused capacity
a server integration run persists exact per-call usage, compact evidence, a plain final answer, and protocol c3
outputArtifacts: agent run/event/token rows in the integration test database
nextSuggestedSteps: If the model repeats an identical call, improve the compact observation rather than adding an autonomous planner.
If the bootstrap grows, move detail behind a mapped handle instead of adding another always-on field.
If a live answer is weak, harvest the objective as a model-led outcome fixture; do not add product-specific query extraction.

promises.claim: Master:frontier C3 model-led execution and retained V1/V2 compatibility gates pass without rebuild work.

promises.claim: Master:frontier V5 has a compact fail-closed coding loop with server-owned restart continuity, exact route/task authority, bounded repository evidence, preconditioned transactional patches, registered checks, and revision-bound diff/test/proof receipts.

server-owned checkpoints are digest, user, session, route-contract, and source-run bound; strict restore rejects every scope mismatch, while a separately validated stale-route replacement accepts only a signed checkpoint whose principal, session, route ID, and source run still match, preserves only its root objective and lineage, starts a zero-revision trajectory without replaying old evidence or authority, records stale_checkpoint_replaced, recalculates current authority through the existing grounded lineage gate, and persists a fresh current-contract checkpoint so contextual continue does not abort; checkpoints omit raw source/diff/output while retaining content-free read SHA receipts so safe edit preconditions bind after restart across canonical relative or absolute route paths; prefix-code operation paths; reject mutations that cannot fit the restart ledger; and reconcile durable action events idempotently

decision, tool, retry, wall-time, route-call, provider-token, and proof-repair counters survive restart; provider usage keeps a bounded recent receipt ring plus durable cumulative calls/tokens that remain exact beyond sixteen calls and drive hard enforcement, pre-call reservation, and final diagnostics; provider diagnostics distinguish durable attempts from successful metered calls and never call missing usage exact; head tokens are per-call, provider/call targets are advisory by default, strict hard budgets require a positive route-owned reservation and subtract the larger host-derived bound over actual messages/tool schemas before enforcing cumulative allowance and measurable usage; zero and sub-second wall limits remain exact; successful retry recovery ends temporary evidence clipping, resets only the consecutive-incident allowance, and never resets the bounded durable total retry count
the avatar browser requests advisory provider accounting rather than claiming a strict input-token cap it cannot measure before dispatch

the exact restart-regression critique remains source-only through the real planner path even when runtime capability exists, completes with the five-call 16211-token quality fixture below its declared advisory target, and a contextual fix inherits mutation authority only from an explicit referential request and the immediately preceding grounded parent on the same user, session, and route; that parent is labeled as the active implementation specification and projected once within the bounded continuity budget without destructive double clipping, while its declared heading outline survives clipping, broad specifications explicitly permit a proved coherent slice with remaining outcomes recorded, and an empty executive checkpoint cannot masquerade as durable progress

source completion requires full-owner or declared-focus coverage, runtime completion requires a scoped snapshot/proof, cross-modality evidence cannot force finalization, host-enforced completion-only mode rejects raw hidden tools, explicit verification intent cannot be downgraded by diagnosis wording, verification requires a passing check plus scoped proof and then deterministically enters completion-only synthesis, implementation requires mutation/check/diff/proof, and evidence cannot short-circuit mutation workflow

MF5/2 removes repeated native schemas, remains below one third of the deterministic legacy base projection, shares one evidence-content budget, retains multiple recent bounded ranges from the same file by path/start/end identity within its fixed twelve-step projection instead of overwriting earlier code context, exposes compact advisory read-overlap, duplicate, and understand/edit/test/diff/prove progress without hidden advisory stops, rejects fully covered reads before execution, and classifies search results with no new source locations as typed non-progress

candidate failures are disqualified individually; the deterministic agent-event fixture proves bounded redacted adapter/lane provenance and terminal-owned trajectory normalization; full normalized sequences cross the lane boundary; golden candidates are extracted only from observed successful motifs supported by at least two agents across three task instances; and strategy candidates stay empty without complete trusted trajectories and observable tool-call counts

implementation planning is an explicit read-only authority class preserved through direct-envelope transport and planner regeneration; it exposes evidence tools plus a model-owned checkpoint but no edit, test, diff, or proof action, and it completes only with a valid operational decision containing state, candidate, target paths, acceptance criterion, blocker when applicable, next action, and bounded confidence rather than hidden reasoning; at its advisory target it enters a durable checkpoint-only two-decision finalization window; the signed restart checkpoint preserves both the executive decision and finalization phase, while a completed final persists the bounded decision and the next turn reconstructs it as one compact MF5/2 decision record

the ordered A-through-E isolation matrix attributes the first missing evidence category across patch capacity, repository navigation, planning-to-execution handoff, prioritization, and multi-turn continuity instead of collapsing every miss into a generic agent failure

autonomous tasks keep a bounded model-authored executive capsule and optional open/done/dropped/blocked outcome ledger across restart; unavailable declared tools convert open outcomes to blocked, actionable open outcomes receive a bounded three-attempt resolution window before durable resumable termination rather than looping; persisted browser turns reconcile run identity and terminal server truth before reconnect streaming, and a missing server run becomes interrupted/resumable instead of remaining pending forever; native tool calls plus plain final text have no competing JSON decision requirement, every native call in a provider batch executes sequentially without invented provider usage, and output-length stops are bounded resumable continuations
the frozen server monolith only delegates repository primitives and passes its growth guard
the proof hashes every declared invalidating input plus the complete owned Python/strategy/browser import closure before and after execution and fails if the input set changes during the run
the proof labels deployed runtime, production provider behavior, and real external-agent trajectory quality as unproven
